recipes.title: Nasi Goreng
recipes.description: Indonesian fried rice with sweet soy sauce, shrimp paste, chillies and shallots, topped with fried egg.
steps: Pound shallots, garlic, chillies and shrimp paste into a smooth paste.
Heat oil in a wok and stir-fry the spice paste until fragrant.
Add diced chicken and cook until browned, then add shrimp.
Add cold cooked rice, breaking up clumps with a spatula.
Drizzle with kecap manis and soy sauce, tossing until rice is evenly colored and slightly charred.
Stir in scallions, then plate up.
Fry an egg sunny-side up for each plate, place on top, and serve with cucumber slices.

recipes.title: Rendang
recipes.description: Slow-cooked Padang beef in coconut milk and spice paste until dry, dark and intensely flavored.
steps: Blend shallots, garlic, galangal, ginger, turmeric and chillies into a smooth paste.
Cook the paste with cinnamon, star anise and cloves in a heavy pot until very fragrant.
Add cubed beef, coconut milk, smashed lemongrass and torn lime and turmeric leaves.
Bring to a simmer and cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 2 hours.
As the liquid reduces, stir more frequently to prevent sticking.
Continue cooking 1 more hour until the sauce is dry, dark brown and fragrantly oily.
Stir in tamarind and salt, then serve with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Sate
recipes.description: Indonesian skewers of marinated chicken or beef grilled over charcoal and served with peanut sauce.
steps: Cut chicken into bite-sized cubes and marinate in kecap manis, soy, garlic, shallots, lime and coriander for 2 hours.
Soak bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes.
Roast peanuts and grind into a coarse paste.
Saute chopped chillies and shallots, add peanut paste, palm sugar, tamarind and coconut milk; simmer until thick.
Thread chicken onto skewers, packing the cubes tightly.
Grill over hot charcoal for 8 minutes, turning and basting with kecap manis until charred.
Serve skewers with warm peanut sauce, sliced shallots and rice cake.

recipes.title: Gado-Gado
recipes.description: Indonesian salad of blanched vegetables, tofu, eggs and rice cake dressed with peanut sauce.
steps: Boil potatoes until tender, then peel and slice; hard boil eggs and halve.
Blanch long beans, cabbage and bean sprouts separately until just tender.
Fry tofu and tempeh in oil until golden, then drain and slice.
Toast peanuts and grind with garlic and chillies into a paste.
Whisk peanut paste with palm sugar, tamarind, kecap manis and water to a thick pourable sauce.
Arrange vegetables, tofu, tempeh, eggs and cucumber on a platter.
Pour peanut sauce over the top and finish with crushed krupuk crackers.

recipes.title: Bakso
recipes.description: Indonesian beef meatball soup in clear broth with noodles, tofu and crispy fried shallots.
steps: Simmer beef bones with crushed garlic and salt for 1.5 hours to make broth.
Blend ground beef with tapioca, garlic, shallots, egg, pepper and a splash of water until very smooth.
Bring a pot of water to a gentle simmer and shape balls with two spoons, dropping them in to cook 5 minutes.
Add tofu and bok choy to the strained broth and warm through.
Cook glass and yellow noodles separately and divide between bowls.
Top with meatballs, vegetables and scallions, then ladle hot broth over.
Finish with fried shallots and a spoon of sambal.

recipes.title: Soto Ayam
recipes.description: Indonesian turmeric chicken soup with vermicelli, hard-boiled egg, lime and fried shallots.
steps: Boil whole chicken in water with bay leaves for 30 minutes; remove, shred meat and reserve broth.
Pound shallots, garlic, turmeric, ginger and candlenuts into a paste.
Saute the paste in oil with lemongrass and lime leaves until fragrant.
Tip the paste into the broth and simmer 15 minutes.
Soak glass noodles, then divide between bowls with bean sprouts and shredded chicken.
Halve hard-boiled eggs and add to bowls.
Ladle hot broth over and finish with lime, fried shallots and sambal.

recipes.title: Mie Goreng
recipes.description: Indonesian fried noodles with chicken, shrimp, vegetables and sweet soy sauce.
steps: Boil noodles until just tender, drain and toss with a little oil.
Pound garlic, shallots and chillies into a paste.
Heat oil in a wok and fry the paste until fragrant.
Add chicken and shrimp, stir-fry until cooked.
Push to one side and scramble eggs in the empty space.
Add cabbage, bok choy and noodles, then drizzle with kecap manis, soy and oyster sauce.
Toss until evenly coated and serve topped with fried shallots.

recipes.title: Pempek
recipes.description: Palembang fish cakes made of mackerel and tapioca, served with dark vinegar-tamarind sauce.
steps: Blend fish with crushed garlic, salt, sugar, pepper and cold water until smooth.
Knead in tapioca starch gradually until a soft dough forms.
Shape into oblongs; for kapal selam, form pouches and stuff with a beaten egg.
Boil pempek until they float, then drain.
Fry boiled pempek in hot oil until golden and crisp on the outside.
For sauce, simmer palm sugar, tamarind, vinegar, water and chopped chillies until syrupy, then strain.
Slice pempek and serve with cucumber and the tangy dark sauce.

recipes.title: Beef Rendang
recipes.description: Minang dry-cooked beef coconut curry with chillies and toasted spice paste, deeply caramelised.
steps: Blend shallots, garlic, galangal, ginger, turmeric and chillies into a paste.
Toast desiccated coconut in a dry pan until golden, then grind to a fine paste.
Cook the spice paste in a heavy pot with smashed lemongrass, lime leaves, turmeric leaves, cinnamon and star anise.
Add cubed brisket, coconut milk and salt; bring to a simmer.
Cook uncovered, stirring often, for 2 hours until liquid reduces and oil starts to separate.
Stir in toasted coconut paste and continue cooking 30 more minutes until very dry and dark brown.
Serve with steamed rice and pickled vegetables.

recipes.title: Nasi Kuning
recipes.description: Indonesian yellow turmeric coconut rice served at celebrations with fried chicken and peanuts.
steps: Rinse rice until water runs clear.
Blend turmeric with a little water and strain to extract bright juice.
Combine rice, coconut milk, turmeric juice, smashed lemongrass, lime leaves, pandan, bay leaves and salt.
Cook in a rice cooker or covered pot with the lid on tight until rice is fluffy.
Hard-boil eggs and halve; fry peanuts in a little oil until crisp.
Mound rice on a platter and surround with fried chicken, eggs, peanuts and cucumber.
Top with fried shallots and serve with sambal.

recipes.title: Rawon
recipes.description: East Javanese black beef soup colored and flavored with kluwek nuts, served with rice and bean sprouts.
steps: Soak kluwek nuts in warm water for 15 minutes, then crack and scoop out the dark flesh.
Simmer cubed beef in water with bay leaves for 1 hour until almost tender.
Blend kluwek flesh, shallots, garlic, candlenuts, galangal, turmeric and chillies into a paste.
Saute the paste in oil with lemongrass and lime leaves until very fragrant.
Stir paste into the beef pot and simmer 30 more minutes.
Season with salt; the broth will turn deep black-brown.
Serve over rice with raw bean sprouts and a wedge of lime.

recipes.title: Gudeg
recipes.description: Yogyakarta jackfruit stew slow-cooked in coconut milk and palm sugar, served with chicken and egg.
steps: Boil young jackfruit chunks in water for 30 minutes, then drain.
Pound shallots, garlic, candlenuts, coriander seeds and galangal into a paste.
Hard-boil eggs and peel.
Layer jackfruit, chicken, eggs, spice paste, bay leaves and teak leaves in a heavy pot.
Pour over coconut milk, palm sugar and salt.
Simmer covered for 4 hours until liquid is reduced and gudeg is dark amber and very tender.
Serve with rice, sambal goreng and krupuk.

recipes.title: Sambal Goreng
recipes.description: Spicy Indonesian stir-fry of liver, tofu and beans in red chilli and coconut milk sambal.
steps: Boil liver until cooked through, then dice; fry tofu cubes until golden.
Blend chillies, shallots, garlic, galangal and tomato into a paste.
Saute paste in oil with lemongrass and lime leaves until fragrant and oil separates.
Add long beans and stir-fry 2 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and bring to a simmer.
Add liver and tofu, season with palm sugar, tamarind and salt.
Simmer 8 minutes until sauce thickens and clings to the ingredients.

recipes.title: Babi Guling
recipes.description: Balinese spit-roasted suckling pig stuffed with spices and cooked until skin is crackling crisp.
steps: Pound shallots, garlic, turmeric, galangal, ginger, coriander, pepper and chillies into the Balinese basa gede paste.
Saute the paste in oil with lemongrass and lime leaves until very fragrant, then cool.
Rub salt all over the pig inside and out.
Stuff the cavity with the basa gede paste, blanched cassava leaves and bay leaves; sew shut.
Truss onto a spit and roast over moderate charcoal for 4 hours, basting with coconut water.
Increase heat for the last 30 minutes to crisp the skin until deep mahogany and crackling.
Rest 20 minutes, then carve and serve with rice and sambal matah.

recipes.title: Ayam Goreng
recipes.description: Indonesian fried chicken marinated in turmeric, garlic and lemongrass, then deep-fried until crisp.
steps: Blend turmeric, garlic, shallots, coriander, candlenuts and galangal into a paste.
Combine chicken pieces in a pot with the paste, smashed lemongrass, lime leaves, bay leaves, salt and coconut water.
Simmer covered until chicken is fully cooked and most liquid is absorbed.
Cool the chicken on a tray and pat dry; reserve any leftover spice bits.
Heat oil to 170C and deep-fry chicken in batches until deeply golden and crisp.
Refry the spice bits separately as crunchy kremes topping.
Serve chicken topped with kremes alongside rice and sambal.

recipes.title: Tempeh Goreng
recipes.description: Indonesian fried tempeh, marinated in coriander and garlic and deep-fried until golden and crisp.
steps: Slice tempeh into thin slabs.
Pound garlic, coriander seeds, salt and turmeric into a paste, then mix with water.
Marinate tempeh in the spice water for 15 minutes.
Heat oil to 170C in a wok or deep pan.
Fry tempeh in batches for 4 minutes until deep golden and crisp on the edges.
Drain on paper towels and serve with sliced bird's eye chillies as a snack or side.
